LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MAXIMO MERCADO AND ALBERTO RAMIREZ'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. (SEE WORD MINUTE FOR DETAILS.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MAXIMO MERCADO AND ALBERTO RAMIREZ'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS FAIL TO CITE AUTHORITY THAT PROHIBITS PLAINTIFF FROM ALLEGING HOLDOVER DAMAGES WHEN A SHELTER-IN-PLACE ORDER IS IN EFFECT, NOR DO THEY SHOW THAT IT WAS IMPOSSIBLE FOR THEM TO MOVE AFTER THEIR TENANCY EXPIRED ON APRIL 24, 2020. ACCORDINGLY, DEFENDANTS DO NOT DEMONSTRATE THAT PARAGRAPH NINETEEN OF THE COMPLAINT AND PARAGRAPH THREE OF PLAINTIFF'S PRAYER FOR RELIEF ARE "NOT DRAWN OR FILED IN CONFORMITY WITH THE LAWS OF THIS STATE, A COURT RULE, OR AN ORDER OF THE COURT." (CODE CIV. PROC., SEC. 436(B).) THE ISSUE OF DAMAGES IS ONE FOR THE TRIER OF FACT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

The motion is granted. The defendant, Quesada, is to appear for the deposition within 20 days of the date of this order and to produce the requested documents. All objections to the document production attached to the deposition notice are waived. No sanctions were sought and none are awarded.

Defendants Maximo Mercado And Alberto Ramirez'S Motion To Strike Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard concurrently:
CGC-20-585658 SHIRLEY YANHUA YU VS. FRANKLIN M. QUESADA ET AL and
CGC-20-585659 SHIRLEY YANHUA YU VS. MARIA ISABEL RIOS ET AL

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Justin Goodman (justin@zfplaw.com) for Plaintiff.
Steve Collier (415-771-9850 x 1122; steve@thclinic.org) of Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Inc. for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Maximo Mercado And Alberto Ramirez'S Motion To Strike Complaint is denied. Defendants fail to cite authority that prohibits Plaintiff from alleging holdover damages when a Shelter-in-Place Order is in effect, nor do they show that it was impossible for them to move after their tenancy expired on April 24, 2020. Accordingly, Defendants do not demonstrate that paragraph nineteen of the Complaint and paragraph three of Plaintiff's Prayer for Relief are "not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court." (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 436(b).) The issue of damages is one for the trier of fact.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
